2605.21683.title: Investigating Concept Alignment Using Implausible Category Members
2605.21683.paper_summary: Developing AI systems with a human-like understanding of everyday concepts is a key step towards developing safe, reliable systems whose behavior makes sense to humans. When probing concept understanding, asking questions about plausible category members (e.g., "Is a car a vehicle?") is likely to recall patterns in the model's vast training data. We pursue an alternative strategy, characterizing the boundaries of conceptual categories by asking about implausible category members (e.g., "Is an olive a vehicle?") to probe the kind of concept-level knowledge we take for granted in fellow humans. We characterize concept boundaries for a set of fundamental concepts by studying AI systems' assignments of objects to superordinate categories from a classic psychological study by Rosch and Mervis, as well as their assignments of the same objects to mismatched superordinate categories. We compare these assignments to those made by human participants on the full range of within-category and cross-category assignment tasks. Our results reveal a range of concepts for which which models differ in meaningful and surprising ways from humans, including treating "words" as belonging to categories like "vehicles" and "clothing," identifying several "vegetable" category members as "fruit," and assigning exemplars from non-weapon categories to the "weapons" category. We also demonstrate how these instances of concept misalignment translate into problematic downstream behavior with implications for AI safety.
2605.21683.paper_summary_zh: 開發具有類人理解日常概念的 AI 系統是邁向開發安全、可靠系統的一個關鍵步驟，這些系統的行為對人類來說是有意義的。當探討概念理解時，詢問有可能的類別成員（例如，“汽車是交通工具嗎？”）可能會喚起模型在其龐大的訓練數據中的模式。我們採取了一種替代策略，通過詢問不太可能的類別成員（例如，“橄欖是交通工具嗎？”）來界定概念類別的邊界，以探測我們在其他人類中理所當然的概念層次知識。我們通過研究 AI 系統將物體分配到超類別的情況，來界定一組基本概念的概念邊界，這些超類別來自 Rosch 和 Mervis 的經典心理學研究，以及它們將相同物體分配到不匹配的超類別的情況。我們將這些分配與人類參與者在全範圍的類別內和跨類別分配任務中的分配進行比較。我們的結果揭示了一系列概念，這些概念在有意義和驚人的方式上與人類存在差異，包括將“單詞”視為屬於“交通工具”和“衣物”等類別，將幾個“蔬菜”類別成員識別為“水果”，以及將來自非武器類別的範例分配到“武器”類別。我們還展示了這些概念不一致的實例如何轉化為具有 AI 安全性影響的問題行為。
2605.21683.author: Sunayana Rane et.al.
2605.21683.authors: Sunayana Rane, Brenden M. Lake, Thomas L. Griffiths
